verdicts.note: Lower taxes support restraint, but the dossier gives no maximal tax-cut mechanism.
Limits on state spending support restraint, but not the maximal pole without a specific cut program.
Public safety is listed without an enforcement-versus-prevention mechanism.
Elected school-board service establishes a local governing record, though not legislative tenure.
Lower taxes support the direction without a maximal cut proposal.
Public safety is only a general priority in the dossier.
Industry policy work supplies relevant institutional experience, making a modest renewal lean better supported than the maximal outsider pole.
Public safety is listed without a documented enforcement or prevention approach.
City-council service establishes an elected local governing record.
Education and health-care priorities do not establish an overall spending posture without a funding mechanism.
Military service and repeat-candidate experience support renewal while making the maximal outsider score too strong.
Public investment is explicit, but the dossier does not document a comprehensive maximal expansion program.
Municipal policy-implementation experience balances the absence of elected tenure.

review_notes: All 16 original candidate-axis pairs were reviewed exactly once (9 adjust, 4 refuted, 3 upheld). No omitted axis had sufficiently specific dossier evidence for a supported missing proposal.
